five_year_trend.key_shift: Introduction of Grade I Signal specific syllabus
Traditional offline to online transition phase

subjects.name: General Awareness & Current Affairs
subjects.analysis: Focus on the intersection of Science and Policy. Railway-specific budget and technology updates are high-yield areas.

topics.name: Current Events
topics.sub_topics: National & International Importance, Sports Awards (Khel Ratna, Arjuna), Summits (G20, BRICS), Military Exercises, New Appointments, Obituary

topics.name: Science & Tech
topics.sub_topics: ISRO Missions (Gaganyaan, Chandrayaan), DRDO Tests, Cyber Security, AI/ML basics, Digital India, Semiconductor Mission

topics.name: Economy & Banking
topics.sub_topics: Union Budget Highlights, Railway Budget integration, GDP Projections, GST Collections, Inflation (WPI/CPI), RBI Repo Rates

topics.name: Culture & History
topics.sub_topics: Indian Freedom Struggle (1857-1947), Classical Dances, UNESCO Heritage Sites in India, Famous Personalities, Tribal Festivals

subjects.name: Mathematics (Comprehensive)
subjects.analysis: Accuracy in calculations is the differentiator. Grade I requires higher-level Algebra while Grade III focuses on pure Arithmetic.

topics.name: Number System
topics.sub_topics: BODMAS, Decimals, Fractions, LCM & HCF, Ratio and Proportion, Rational & Irrational numbers, Divisibility Rules, Surds and Indices

topics.name: Arithmetic Operations
topics.sub_topics: Percentages, Profit and Loss, Simple and Compound Interest, Time and Work (Pipes & Cisterns), Time and Distance (Trains, Boats & Streams), Average, Partnership

topics.name: Algebra & Geometry
topics.sub_topics: Elementary Algebra, Linear Equations, Quadratic Equations, Polynomials, Lines and Angles, Triangles, Circles, Coordinate Geometry

topics.name: Mensuration
topics.sub_topics: Area and Perimeter of 2D shapes, Volume and Surface Area of 3D shapes (Cylinder, Cone, Sphere, Cube), Frustum

topics.name: Trigonometry & Statistics
topics.sub_topics: Trigonometric Ratios, Heights and Distances, Mean, Median, Mode, Standard Deviation, Frequency Distribution, Pie Charts

subjects.name: General Intelligence & Reasoning
subjects.analysis: Non-verbal reasoning and logical deductions carry maximum weight. Practice speed-reading of complex puzzles.

topics.name: Logical Reasoning
topics.sub_topics: Analogies, Alphabetical and Number Series, Coding and Decoding, Mathematical Operations, Relationships (Blood Relations), Syllogism

topics.name: Analytical Reasoning
topics.sub_topics: Jumbling, Venn Diagram, Data Interpretation and Sufficiency, Conclusions and Decision Making, Similarities and Differences

topics.name: Spatial Reasoning
topics.sub_topics: Classification, Directions, Statement-Arguments and Assumptions, Paper Folding & Cutting, Mirror Images, Embedded Figures

subjects.name: Basic Science and Engineering (Grade III)
subjects.analysis: This is the 'Selection Section'. It covers the ITI/Trade level concepts and core Physics. High depth required.

topics.name: Engineering Drawing
topics.sub_topics: Projections, Views, Drawing Instruments, Lines, Geometric Figures, Symbolic Representation

topics.name: Units and Measurements
topics.sub_topics: SI Units, Derived Units, Dimensions, Mass, Weight and Density, Speed and Velocity, Work, Power and Energy

topics.name: Thermal & Electrical
topics.sub_topics: Heat and Temperature, Pressure, Basic Electricity (Ohm's Law, Kirchoff's Law), Resistance in Series/Parallel, Magnetic effect of current

topics.name: Occupational Safety
topics.sub_topics: Health and Safety, Environment Education, IT Literacy (Hardware, OS, Networking, MS Office), First Aid, Fire Safety

subjects.name: Advanced Physics & Math (Grade I Signal)
subjects.analysis: Highly technical. Requires 12th Std/Diploma level understanding of Electronics and Applied Mathematics.

topics.name: Electronics & Magnetism
topics.sub_topics: Semiconductors (Diodes, Transistors), Digital Electronics (Logic Gates, Number Systems), Electromagnetic Induction, Capacitance, AC Circuits

topics.name: Computer & IT
topics.sub_topics: Computer Architecture, Programming Basics, Networking Protocols (TCP/IP, OSI Layers), Data Structures, Storage Devices

mentorship_data.title: Trade-Specific Focus
mentorship_data.content: For Grade III, your merit is tied to Basic Science. Do not ignore Engineering Drawing; 5 marks here often decide the final cutoff.

mentorship_data.title: The 1/3 Negative Rule
mentorship_data.content: Railway marking is harsher than Banking. Avoid 'blind guessing'. Attempt only if 75% sure of the logic.

mentorship_data.title: Medical Standards
mentorship_data.content: Technician B1/B2 standards are strict regarding color blindness and LASIK. Get a pre-check done before Phase I.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Can I apply for both Grade I and Grade III?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Yes, if you meet the eligibility criteria for both, you must submit separate applications and pay separate fees.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is the syllabus same for all Technician trades?
frequently_asked_questions.a: The CBT pattern is common, but technical weightage varies between Grade I (Signal) and Grade III (Workshop/Other Trades).

frequently_asked_questions.q: What is the qualifying mark for RRB Technician?
frequently_asked_questions.a: UR/EWS: 40%, OBC/SC: 30%, ST: 25%. However, actual cutoffs are much higher based on competition.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is there a Skill Test or Trade Test?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Unlike previous years, the current notification focuses on a single-stage CBT followed by DV and Medical.
Yes, Percentile-based normalization is used if the exam is conducted in multiple shifts to ensure fairness.
